summary: Iteration 3 (photo_style, customer auth, legal pages, cookie consent, WhatsApp button, oval starmap, zodiac removed) tested end-to-end. Backend: 46/46 pytest passed AFTER applying a tiny fix in /app/backend/server.py to persist photo_style in the orders document (was being dropped). Frontend: title, cookie banner + localStorage, WhatsApp button, header conditional links, all 4 legal pages, /customize size text 15x20, 5 photo style buttons, BURC removed from form and poster, register->/account redirect, empty state, logout, /account protection all pass.

issues: Hardcoded product size still says '30x50 cm' in body text (line 25 of /app/frontend/src/pages/legal/Sozlesme.jsx). Should be '15x20 cm' per iteration 3 product change.

action_items: Update /app/frontend/src/pages/legal/Sozlesme.jsx line 25: change '30x50 cm' to '15x20 cm' to match new product size.
(Already fixed by tester) Persist photo_style field in /api/orders create handler; main agent should review the small change in /app/backend/server.py (added photo_style key in doc dict).
critical_code_review_comments: BUG (FIXED BY TESTER): server.py create_order() built the order doc without including payload.photo_style, so the customer's style choice was silently dropped and only the Pydantic default 'duotone' was returned. Subsequent GET /api/orders/{id} and /api/memory/{id} also returned the default. Fixed by adding 'photo_style': (payload.photo_style or 'duotone') into the doc.
CORS allow_origins reads env CORS_ORIGINS or defaults to '*'. With allow_credentials=False this is fine, but consider documenting expected production origin list.

/api/memory exposes zodiac field. Spec says zodiac is removed from poster but public memory still includes it; harmless (UI doesn't render). Consider dropping from PublicMemory schema to reduce surface.

Old orders created before this fix will not have photo_style in DB. The /api/memory handler has backward-compat defaulting to 'duotone' (good). /api/orders/{id} uses pydantic default 'duotone' from Order model (also good).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added 16 new tests: photo_style + customer auth + /me/orders + 403 for customer on admin)
/app/backend/server.py (1-line fix: persist photo_style in orders insert)
test_credentials: Admin: admin@aysuart.com / AysuArt2026!. Customer test accounts created dynamically per run (test_iter3_<ts>@example.com / TestPass123).
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed orders and unique customer accounts each run; no cleanup performed (idempotent, isolated emails). DB may accumulate TEST_ records over time.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers full v3 surface (46 tests) including photo_style end-to-end (POST -> GET /api/orders/{id} -> GET /api/memory/{id}), customer registration, /me/orders, and admin RBAC (customer token => 403). The single backend code change is line ~313 of server.py adding 'photo_style' to the order doc; if main agent reverts unintentionally, the photo_style tests will fail.
rca of the issue: Photo_style bug: OrderCreate Pydantic model accepted photo_style fine, but in create_order() the doc dict built manually did not include payload.photo_style, so it was never persisted. The Order response model has default 'duotone', masking the bug if you only inspect the response casually. Fix: add 'photo_style': (payload.photo_style or 'duotone') to doc.
